title: Reader Outcomes, Publisher Stewardship, and Service Charter · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Publisher Stewardship
summary: A coalition asks for a blog aggregator but has not named its readers, publisher obligations, freshness promise, moderation boundary, accessibility needs, or evidence of usefulness. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go publisher stewardship increment in reader-centered feed-service charter. Learners progress through prediction, expl…
objectives: Define respectful fetch frequency, identification, caching, opt-out, correction, deletion, and incident contacts for publishers.
Define reader group, decision, task journey, freshness need, harm, and observable value before choosing architecture.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Publisher Stewardship · Theory Feed Go Publisher Stewardship
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 9730 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate a useful reader and publisher service charter on observable decisions and inclusive evidence; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define respectful fetch frequency, identification, caching, opt-out, correction, deletion, and incident contacts for publishers.
